name: Charca Grande Roman Dam Loop from Esparragalejo
name_original: Presa Romana "Charca Grande" — circular desde Esparragalejo
description: This moderate 4.5-mile (7.2 km) jogging route from Esparragalejo features paved tracks and the ancient Roman Dam "Charca Grande".
region_intro: Jogging routes around Esparragalejo often feature paths near historical Roman dams and reservoirs, providing a blend of natural and ancient landscapes. The terrain is generally gentle, with minimal elevation changes, making it suitable for various fitness levels. This region offers a mix of open routes and trails that may pass through agricultural areas or along water bodies. Best jogging routes around Esparragalejo The most popular jogging route is Ride to the Prosperina Reservoir, a 3.5 miles (5.7 km) trail that takes 36 minutes to complete, offering views of the reservoir. Another top favourite among local runners is Charca Grande Roman Dam Loop from Esparragalejo, a moderate 4.5 miles (7.2 km) path. This route circles the historic Roman dam, providing a mix of open and waterside scenery. Local runners also love the Proserpina Roman Dam – Roman Dam of Proserpina loop from Embalse de Proserpina, a 7.2 miles (11.7 km) trail leading through areas surrounding the Roman Dam of Proserpina, often completed in about 1 hour 14 minutes. Jogging around Esparragalejo is defined by routes near reservoirs, Roman dams, and open countryside. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and challenging running, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Esparragalejo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 7 reviews. More than 900 runners have used komoot to explore Esparragalejo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Esparragalejo?
A: There are over 70 running routes available in the Esparragalejo area, catering to various fitness levels. These include options for easy, moderate, and more challenging runs.

Q: Are there easy running routes suitable for beginners in Esparragalejo?
A: Yes, Esparragalejo offers several easy running routes perfect for beginners. The terrain is generally gentle with minimal elevation changes. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992989932/ride-to-the-prosperina-reservoir" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ride to the Prosperina Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 3.5-mile (5.7 km) trail.

Q: Where can I find longer running trails around Esparragalejo?
A: For longer distances, consider routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40324392" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Proserpina Roman Dam – Roman Dam of Proserpina loop from Embalse de Proserpina&lt;/a&gt;. This trail is 7.2 miles (11.7 km) long and takes about 1 hour 14 minutes to complete, offering a more extended running experience.

Q: Are there circular running routes available in Esparragalejo?
A: Yes, many routes around Esparragalejo are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1280660340/charca-grande-roman-dam-loop-from-esparragalejo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Charca Grande Roman Dam Loop from Esparragalejo&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 4.5-mile (7.2 km) circular path that circles the historic Roman dam.

Q: What can I see along the running routes in Esparragalejo?
A: Running routes in Esparragalejo often feature historical Roman dams and reservoirs, providing a blend of natural and ancient landscapes. You can encounter landmarks such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3350184" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Proserpina Roman Dam&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6309612" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Proserpina Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6059624" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Dam "Charca Grande"&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes may also pass through agricultural areas.

Q: Are there any challenging running routes for experienced runners?
A: While the terrain is generally gentle, Esparragalejo does offer more challenging options. There are 22 difficult routes available, which may feature longer distances or slightly more varied elevation, providing a good workout for experienced runners.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Esparragalejo?
A: The best time for running in Esparragalejo is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) months. Temperatures are milder and more comfortable for outdoor activities. Summers can be very hot, and winters are generally mild but can be damp.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Esparragalejo?
A: Many outdoor trails in the Esparragalejo region are dog-friendly, especially those in open countryside or along reservoirs. However, it's always advisable to keep your dog on a leash, especially near agricultural areas or wildlife, and to check local regulations for specific routes or protected areas.

Q: Are there family-friendly running paths in Esparragalejo?
A: Yes, the generally gentle terrain and minimal elevation changes make many routes suitable for families. Paths near reservoirs or through open countryside offer pleasant and accessible options for running with children, though specific amenities like playgrounds might not be directly on the trails.

Q: What do other runners enjoy the most about jogging in Esparragalejo?
A: The running routes in Esparragalejo are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from 7 reviews. Runners often praise the blend of natural scenery with historical Roman structures, the varied terrain from open countryside to waterside paths, and the options available for different ability levels.

Q: How can I manage running during hot weather in Esparragalejo?
A: During the hotter months, it's recommended to run early in the morning or late in the evening to avoid the midday heat. Choose routes with some shade if possible, stay well-hydrated, wear light clothing, and consider shorter distances. The region's open routes can be exposed to the sun.

Q: Are there running routes with cafes or refreshment stops nearby?
A: While many routes traverse natural and historical landscapes, some may pass close to towns or villages where cafes and shops are available. For routes near the larger reservoirs or dams, there might be facilities, but it's best to carry your own water and snacks, especially on longer or more remote trails.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this jogging route?
faqs.answer: The "Presa Romana 'Charca Grande' — circular desde Esparragalejo" route is considered moderate for jogging. While it's not overly strenuous, it does require a good level of physical fitness to comfortably complete the approximately 7.2 km distance.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail primarily features paved tracks (pistas pavimentadas), making for a relatively smooth jogging surface. There may also be some accessible paths (sendas accesibles) along the route.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The most significant feature is the well-preserved Roman Dam "Charca Grande", dating back to the 1st and 2nd centuries A.D. You'll also start and end your journey in the charming settlement of Esparragalejo.

faqs.question: Where can I park when visiting the trail?
faqs.answer: Since the route is a circular trail starting and ending in Esparragalejo, it's recommended to look for parking within the town. Specific parking areas are not detailed, but you should find suitable spots in or near the settlement.

faqs.question: Is the Presa Romana "Charca Grande" trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific rules aren't provided, generally, well-behaved dogs on a leash are welcome on public trails. Please ensure you clean up after your pet and respect other users and local wildlife.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to use this trail?
faqs.answer: No, typically, no permits or entrance fees are required to access and enjoy public outdoor trails like the "Presa Romana 'Charca Grande'" route.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: The region of Extremadura can experience hot summers. Therefore, the best times to jog this route are generally during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder and the natural scenery is vibrant. Early mornings or late afternoons are also preferable during warmer months.

faqs.question: Is this a loop trail or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: As indicated by its name, "circular desde Esparragalejo," this is a loop trail. You will start and finish your jog in the same location, Esparragalejo.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: The trail's name, "Charca Grande" (large pond), suggests a scenic natural setting, likely featuring a reservoir or large body of water. The surrounding region of Extremadura is known for its diverse natural landscapes, including forests of chestnut, oak, and holm-oak trees, offering appealing views.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the jogging route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 7.2 km long. For an average jogger, it typically takes around 50 minutes to complete the entire loop.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restrooms near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Since the trail starts and ends in Esparragalejo, you should be able to find basic facilities such as cafes, shops, or restrooms within the town before or after your jog.
